input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Battery`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Battery` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.6.6.5
            TITLE: battery packs with low voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.6.5: Battery packs with Low Voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be
EV.6.6.5 Battery packs with Low Voltage or non voltage rated fusible links for cell connections may be 
used when all three conditions are met:   
• An Overcurrent Protection device rated at less than or equal to one third the sum of the 
parallel fusible links and complying with EV.6.6.2.b above is connected in series.  
• The AMS can detect an open fusible link and will Open the Shutdown Circuit EV.7.2.2 if a 
fault is detected. 
• Fusible link current rating is specified in manufacturer’s data or suitable test data is 
provided.

RULE: f.11.1.3
            TITLE: tractive system (ev.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in f.11.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.1.3: Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2
F.11.1.3 Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2 
above must be contained inside one or both of: 
• The Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13 
• Structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: t.1.8.1
            TITLE: requirement

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.1: Requirement
T.1.8.1 Requirement 
A Firewall(s) must separate the driver compartment and any portion of the Driver Harness 
from:  
a. All components of the Fuel System, the engine oil, the liquid cooling systems, any lithium 
batteries 
b. (EV only) All Tractive System components other than Outboard Wheel Motors EV.4.1.3 
where mounted at the wheels or on the front control arms

RULE: ev.4.3.8
            TITLE: each accumulator container must be labelled with the:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.3.8: Each Accumulator Container must be labelled with the:
EV.4.3.8 Each Accumulator Container must be labelled with the:  
a. School Name and Vehicle Number  
b. Symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black lightning bolt on yellow 
background) with triangle side length of 100 mm minimum 
c. Text “Always Energized”  
d. Text “High Voltage” if the voltage meets T.9.1.1

RULE: d.3.6
            TITLE: starting

            CONTENT:
            rule D.3.6: Starting
D.3.6 Starting 
Auxiliary batteries must not be used once a vehicle has moved to the starting line of any 
event.  See IC.8.1

RULE: ev.5.9.3
            TITLE: when the voltage outside the accumulator container(s) exceeds t.9.1.1, the tsal must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.9.3: When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) exceeds T.9.1.1, the TSAL must:
EV.5.9.3 When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) exceeds T.9.1.1, the TSAL must: 
a. Be Color:  Red 
b. Flash with a frequency between 2 Hz and 5 Hz

RULE: ev.8.2.1
            TITLE: the charger must be galvanically isolated (ac) input to (dc) output.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.8.2.1: The Charger must be galvanically isolated (AC) input to (DC) output.
EV.8.2.1 The Charger must be galvanically isolated (AC) input to (DC) output.

RULE: t.1.7.1
            TITLE: all vehicles must have a floor closeout to prevent track debris from entering

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.1: All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering
T.1.7.1 All vehicles must have a Floor Closeout to prevent track debris from entering

RULE: t.9.2.2
            TITLE: all low voltage batteries must have overcurrent protection that trips at or below the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.2.2: All Low Voltage batteries must have Overcurrent Protection that trips at or below the
T.9.2.2 All Low Voltage batteries must have Overcurrent Protection that trips at or below the 
maximum specified discharge current of the cells

RULE: t.1.7.4
            TITLE: if multiple panels are used, gaps between panels must not exceed 3 mm

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.4: If multiple panels are used, gaps between panels must not exceed 3 mm
T.1.7.4 If multiple panels are used, gaps between panels must not exceed 3 mm

RULE: t.1.7.3
            TITLE: the panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.7.3: The panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material
T.1.7.3 The panel(s) must be made of a solid, non brittle material

RULE: ev.4.4.4
            TITLE: low voltage batteries must meet t.9.2

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.4.4: Low Voltage Batteries must meet T.9.2
EV.4.4.4 Low Voltage Batteries must meet T.9.2
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 92 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: ev.8.2.3
            TITLE: all connections of the charger(s) must be isolated and covered.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.8.2.3: All connections of the Charger(s) must be isolated and covered.
EV.8.2.3 All connections of the Charger(s) must be isolated and covered.

RULE: ev.7.3.1
            TITLE: an accumulator management system must monitor the accumulator(s) voltage ev.7.4 and

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.3.1: An Accumulator Management System must monitor the Accumulator(s) Voltage EV.7.4 and
EV.7.3.1 An Accumulator Management System must monitor the Accumulator(s) Voltage EV.7.4 and 
Temperature EV.7.5 when the:  
a. Tractive System is Active  EV.11.5 
b. Accumulator is connected to a Charger  EV.8.3

RULE: ev.8.4.1
            TITLE: when charging, the ams and imd must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.8.4.1: When Charging, the AMS and IMD must:
EV.8.4.1 When Charging, the AMS and IMD must: 
a. Monitor the Accumulator  
b. Open the Charging Shutdown Circuit if a fault is detected.

RULE: ev.5.9.4
            TITLE: when the voltage outside the accumulator container(s) is below t.9.1.1, the tsal must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.9.4: When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) is below T.9.1.1, the TSAL must:
EV.5.9.4 When the voltage outside the Accumulator Container(s) is below T.9.1.1, the TSAL must: 
a. Be Color:  Green 
b. Stay continuously illuminated

RULE: t.1.4.4
            TITLE: all vehicle controls must stay below the top-most point of the front hoop in any operational

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.4: All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational
T.1.4.4 All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational 
position

RULE: t.1.4.3
            TITLE: all vehicle controls (steering, gear change, cockpit main switch / cockpit shutdown button)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.3: All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button)
T.1.4.3 All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button) 
must be operated from inside the cockpit without any part of the driver, including hands, arms 
or elbows, being outside of: 
a. The Side Impact Structure defined in F.6.4 / F.7.5 
b. Two longitudinal vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the 
uppermost member of the Side Impact Structure

RULE: ev.11.5.3
            TITLE: a team member(s) who has knowledge of the charging process must stay with the

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.11.5.3: A team member(s) who has knowledge of the Charging process must stay with the
EV.11.5.3 A team member(s) who has knowledge of the Charging process must stay with the 
Accumulator(s) during Charging
